title: Decode Ways
problem_statement: Given a digit string, count the number of ways to decode it using mapping 1->A, 2->B, ..., 26->Z.
constraints: s contains only digits and may include leading zeros
intuition: DP on string positions, considering one-digit and two-digit decode options.

approaches.name: Linear DP with small-state transitions
approaches.description: Traverse the string and build the count of decodings using previous one and two positions.
approaches.why_not: Brute force decoding branches can explode exponentially.

interview_qa.q: How do zeros affect decode counts?
interview_qa.a: A zero must be paired with a previous 1 or 2; standalone zeros are invalid.
dp[i] = dp[i-1] if current digit is valid + dp[i-2] if last two digits form a valid number.
